description:
Full infra tests show `infra/src/lambda/mdi-intake.ts` is stale relative to the shared `submitMdiIntake` contract. The Lambda tests still inject/use `submitResponses`, while the shared service now expects the case-creation/handoff contract and rejects missing `casePayload` with `invalid_input` 400.

Scope
- Decide whether the launch Lambda should submit questionnaire responses through the current shared `createCase` handoff path or a separate response-submit service.
- Update `infra/src/lambda/mdi-intake.ts` and `infra/test/mdi-intake-lambda.test.ts` to match the approved shared contract.
- Preserve thin-PHI behavior: do not persist questionnaire answers, raw MDI payloads, workflow URLs/tokens, or clinical content.
- Keep MDI retry/idempotency behavior deterministic and patient-safe.

Acceptance
- `npm test --prefix infra` passes.
- Submit tests prove transient answers are sent only to MDI, not written to DynamoDB/logs.
- Provider unavailable/maintenance returns patient-safe errors and does not duplicate MDI patient/case creation.
title: Repair MDI intake Lambda submit contract drift
